title: Run With a Read-Only Root Filesystem
description:
## Make the Container Filesystem Immutable

A read-only root filesystem stops a compromised process from modifying the container — a strong hardening step for stateless services.

**Requirements:**
1. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`hardened`** in the background (keep it alive)
2. Give it a **read-only root filesystem** (`--read-only`)

> 💡 `docker run -d --name hardened --read-only alpine sleep 3600`. Verify with `docker inspect -f '{{.HostConfig.ReadonlyRootfs}}' hardened`.

hints.title: Enable a read-only rootfs
hints.body: The --read-only flag mounts the container's root filesystem read-only. Add --tmpfs /tmp if the app needs scratch space.

validation.description: Checks that 'hardened' is running with a read-only root filesystem.
